evals.prompt: Draft the Introduction for my manuscript on transformer models for ECG arrhythmia detection. I want flowing paragraphs, not bullet points — establish the context, the gap, and our objectives.
evals.expected_output: Should trigger the scientific-writing skill. Produces an IMRAD Introduction in full flowing prose paragraphs (never bullet points), establishing context, identifying the gap, and stating objectives. Uses the two-stage outline-then-prose approach and grounds claims in literature via research-lookup.

assertions.value: Writes the Introduction as full flowing paragraphs (not bullet points), establishing context, gap, and objectives in IMRAD form.

evals.prompt: Help me write the Results section of my randomized controlled trial manuscript, and make sure the reporting follows CONSORT.
evals.expected_output: Should trigger. Drafts a Results section that reports findings objectively (primary then secondary outcomes, effect sizes with statistics, integration with figures/tables) in prose, and applies the CONSORT reporting guideline appropriate to an RCT.

assertions.value: Writes objective Results prose and applies the CONSORT reporting guideline for the randomized controlled trial.

evals.prompt: Write a 250-word structured abstract for my observational cohort study and format the in-text citations in AMA style.
evals.expected_output: Should trigger. Produces a structured abstract within the word limit capturing purpose/methods/results/conclusions, and applies AMA citation style (numbered superscript). May note STROBE relevance for the observational design.

assertions.value: Writes a structured abstract at the requested length and formats in-text citations in AMA style.

evals.prompt: My Discussion is a wall of vague, wordy sentences. Tighten it for clarity and conciseness while keeping it as flowing prose, and make sure it relates results to prior work and states limitations honestly.
evals.expected_output: Should trigger. Revises the Discussion for clarity, conciseness, and precision while preserving flowing prose (no bullet points), ensuring it relates findings to prior literature, acknowledges limitations honestly, and suggests implications/future directions.

assertions.value: Improves clarity and conciseness of the Discussion as flowing prose, relating results to prior work and stating limitations.

evals.prompt: I'm submitting to NeurIPS this year — give me the official LaTeX template, the page-limit and formatting rules, and the venue's house style for the abstract.
evals.expected_output: Should NOT trigger scientific-writing. Supplying a specific venue's LaTeX template, formatting/page-limit rules, and house style is the alterlab-venue-templates skill's territory. Per this skill's own guidance, venue-specific formatting and style should defer to venue-templates; scientific-writing covers general IMRAD/clarity/citation principles, not venue boilerplate.

evals.prompt: Find the papers I cite, pull accurate metadata from Google Scholar and PubMed, and generate clean validated BibTeX entries for my reference manager.
evals.expected_output: Should NOT trigger scientific-writing. Searching databases for papers, extracting metadata, and generating/validating BibTeX entries is the alterlab-citation-mgmt skill's territory, not drafting manuscript prose. The correct response defers to citation-mgmt; scientific-writing applies citation styles in text but does not build the BibTeX library.
